name: Flowchart
description: Decision flow with branches and shapes (interactive canvas).
code:
flowchart TD
  Start(["Start"]) --> Init["Initialize"]
  Init --> Cond{"Valid input?"}
  Cond -->|Yes| Work["Process data"]
  Cond -->|No| Err["Log error"]
  Work --> Done(["Finish"])
  Err --> Done

  classDef good fill:#22c55e,stroke:#15803d
  classDef bad fill:#ef4444,stroke:#991b1b
  class Done good
  class Err bad

name: Microservices
description: API gateway fanning out to services and stores.
code:
flowchart LR
  Client(["Client App"]) --> GW["API Gateway"]
  subgraph svc ["Services"]
    Auth["Auth Service"]
    Users["User Service"]
    Orders["Order Service"]
  end
  GW --> Auth
  GW --> Users
  GW --> Orders
  Users --> UDB[("User DB")]
  Orders --> ODB[("Order DB")]
  Orders -.->|events| Bus{{"Event Bus"}}
  Bus -.-> Notify["Notification Service"]

  classDef store fill:#f59e0b,stroke:#b45309
  class UDB,ODB store

name: CI/CD Pipeline
description: Build, test, and deploy with a failure branch.
code:
flowchart LR
  Commit(["Git Commit"]) --> Build["Build Image"]
  Build --> Test{"Tests pass?"}
  Test -->|Yes| Push["Push to Registry"]
  Test -->|No| Alert["Slack Alert"]
  Push --> Deploy["Deploy to K8s"]
  Deploy --> Done(("Live"))

  classDef ok fill:#22c55e,stroke:#15803d
  classDef warn fill:#ef4444,stroke:#991b1b
  class Done ok
  class Alert warn

name: Sequence Diagram
description: Interaction between participants over time.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  participant U as User
  participant A as API
  participant DB as Database
  U->>A: POST /login
  A->>DB: SELECT user
  DB-->>A: user row
  A-->>U: 200 OK + token
  Note over U,A: Session established

name: Class Diagram
description: Object-oriented structure with inheritance.

name: State Diagram
description: Lifecycle states and transitions.
code:
stateDiagram-v2
  [*] --> Idle
  Idle --> Loading: fetch
  Loading --> Success: ok
  Loading --> Error: fail
  Success --> [*]
  Error --> Idle: retry

    int id

name: AWS Cloud Architecture
description: Multi-AZ AWS deployment with ALB, ECS, Aurora, ElastiCache, and CDN.
code:
flowchart LR
  User(["User Browser"]) --> CDN["CloudFront CDN"]
  CDN --> ALB["Application\nLoad Balancer"]

  subgraph vpc ["VPC — us-east-1"]
    subgraph pub ["Public Subnets"]
      ALB
      NAT["NAT Gateway"]
    end
    subgraph app ["App Tier — Private"]
      ECS1["ECS Container A"]
      ECS2["ECS Container B"]
    end
    subgraph data ["Data Tier — Isolated"]
      RDS[("Aurora PostgreSQL Primary")]
      RDSr[("Aurora PostgreSQL Read Replica")]
      Cache[("ElastiCache Redis Cluster")]
    end
  end

  S3[("S3 Static Assets")] --- CDN
  ALB --> ECS1 & ECS2
  ECS1 & ECS2 --> RDS & Cache
  RDS --> RDSr
  ECS1 & ECS2 --> NAT --> Internet(["Internet"])

  classDef aws fill:#ff990020,stroke:#ff9900,color:#ff9900
  classDef infra fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef datastore fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399
  classDef user fill:#7c3aed20,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#c4b5fd
  class CDN,ALB,NAT aws
  class ECS1,ECS2 infra
  class RDS,RDSr,Cache,S3 datastore
  class User user

  CI -->|build and scan| Registry["Container Registry"]

  subgraph K8s ["Kubernetes Cluster"]

      API["API Deployment\n3 replicas"]
      Worker["Worker Deployment\n2 replicas"]
      HPA["HorizontalPodAutoscaler"]

      Ingress["NGINX Ingress"]
      Vault["Vault Agent Injector"]
    end
    subgraph ns_obs ["namespace: observability"]
      Prom["Prometheus"]
      Grafana["Grafana"]
    end
  end

  Registry --> API & Worker
  Ingress --> API
  HPA --> API
  API --> Worker
  Prom --> Grafana
  API & Worker --> Prom
  Vault --> API & Worker

  classDef ci fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef wl fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399
  classDef infra fill:#7c3aed20,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#c4b5fd
  classDef obs fill:#d9770620,stroke:#d97706,color:#fbbf24

  class API,Worker,HPA wl

name: Event-Driven Microservices
description: Kafka-based async microservices with per-service databases and API gateway.
code:
flowchart LR
  Client(["Client"]) --> GW["API Gateway\n(Kong)"]

  subgraph services ["Core Services"]
    Order["Order Service"]
    Inventory["Inventory Service"]
    Payment["Payment Service"]
    Notify["Notification Service"]
    Fulfil["Fulfillment Service"]
  end

  subgraph bus ["Event Bus — Apache Kafka"]
    T1{{"order.created"}}
    T2{{"payment.processed"}}
    T3{{"inventory.reserved"}}
  end

  subgraph stores ["Data Stores"]
    ODB[("Order DB PostgreSQL")]
    IDB[("Inventory DB MySQL")]
    PDB[("Payment DB PostgreSQL")]
    RedisCache[("Cache Redis")]
  end

  GW --> Order & Inventory & Payment
  Order --> ODB
  Payment --> PDB
  Inventory --> IDB
  GW --> RedisCache

  Order -->|publish| T1
  Payment -->|publish| T2
  Inventory -->|publish| T3

  T1 -->|consume| Payment & Inventory
  T2 -->|consume| Fulfil & Notify
  T3 -->|consume| Fulfil

  classDef svc fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef topic fill:#7c3aed20,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#c4b5fd
  classDef db fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399

  class T1,T2,T3 topic
  class ODB,IDB,PDB,RedisCache db

name: Zero Trust Security
description: Zero-trust network access with policy engine and continuous verification signals.
code:
flowchart LR
  User["User\n(Any device)"] --> IdP["Identity Provider\n(Okta / AzureAD)"]
  IdP -->|MFA verified| PEP["Policy Enforcement\nPoint"]
  PEP -->|request context| PE["Policy Engine\n(OPA)"]
  PE -->|trust score| PEP
  PEP -->|allowed| AppA["Internal App A"]
  PEP -->|allowed| AppB["Internal App B"]
  PEP -->|denied| Block["Blocked"]

  subgraph signals ["Continuous Verification"]
    DevPos["Device Posture\n(EDR / MDM)"]
    NetCtx["Network Context\n(IP Reputation)"]
    Beh["Behaviour Analytics\n(UEBA)"]
  end

  DevPos & NetCtx & Beh --> PE

  AppA --> DB1[("Sensitive Data Store")]
  AppB --> DB2[("PII Database")]
  DB1 & DB2 --> DLP["DLP Scanner"]

  classDef auth fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef signal fill:#d9770620,stroke:#d97706,color:#fbbf24
  classDef appcls fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399
  classDef deny fill:#dc262620,stroke:#dc2626,color:#f87171
  class IdP,PEP,PE auth
  class DevPos,NetCtx,Beh signal
  class AppA,AppB,DB1,DB2,DLP appcls
  class Block deny

name: DevSecOps Pipeline
description: Security-gated CI/CD: SAST, SCA, container scanning, signing, and GitOps deploy.
code:
flowchart LR
  Dev["Developer"] -->|commit| Repo["Git Repository"]
  Repo -->|PR trigger| Gates

  subgraph Gates ["Quality and Security Gates"]
    Lint["Lint + Format"]
    SAST["SAST\n(Semgrep)"]
    Tests["Unit + Integration\nTests"]
    SCA["Dependency Scan\n(Snyk)"]
  end

  Lint & SAST & Tests & SCA -->|all pass| Build["Container Build\n(Buildkit)"]
  Build --> ImgScan["Image Scan\n(Trivy)"]
  ImgScan -->|clean| Sign["Image Sign\n(Cosign)"]
  Sign --> Registry["OCI Registry\n(Harbor)"]
  Registry -->|GitOps| ArgoCD["ArgoCD Sync"]
  ArgoCD --> Staging["Staging Kubernetes"]
  Staging --> DAST["DAST\n(OWASP ZAP)"]
  DAST -->|approved| Prod["Production Kubernetes"]
  Prod --> Monitor["Grafana Observability"]

  classDef dev fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef sec fill:#dc262620,stroke:#dc2626,color:#f87171
  classDef build fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399
  classDef deploy fill:#d9770620,stroke:#d97706,color:#fbbf24
  class Dev,Repo dev
  class Lint,SAST,Tests,SCA,ImgScan,Sign,DAST sec
  class Build,Registry build
  class ArgoCD,Staging,Prod,Monitor deploy

name: Incident Response Runbook
description: SEV-1/2/3 triage, war room, investigation, fix verification, and PIR process.
code:
flowchart TD
  Alert["Alert Fired\nPagerDuty / OpsGenie"] --> Ack["On-call Engineer\nAcknowledges"]
  Ack --> Sev{"Severity?"}

  Sev -->|SEV-1 Customer Impact| War["Open War Room\nZoom + Slack"]
  Sev -->|SEV-2 Degraded| Inv["Investigate"]
  Sev -->|SEV-3 Minor| Ticket["Create Ticket\n& Monitor"]

  War --> IC["Assign Incident Commander"]
  IC --> Diag["Diagnose\nLogs + Traces + Metrics"]
  Inv --> Diag

  Diag --> Root{"Root Cause\nFound?"}
  Root -->|Yes| Fix["Apply Fix / Rollback\n/ Feature Flag Off"]
  Root -->|No| Esc["Escalate to\nDomain Expert"]
  Esc --> Diag

  Fix --> Verify["Verify Resolution\nMonitor 10 min"]
  Verify --> OK{"Resolved?"}
  OK -->|Yes| Comms["Post Resolution\nCommunication"]
  OK -->|No| Diag

  Comms --> PIR["Post-Incident Review\nwithin 48h"]
  PIR --> Actions["Track Action Items"]
  Ticket --> Actions

  classDef sev1 fill:#dc262620,stroke:#dc2626,color:#f87171
  classDef sev2 fill:#d9770620,stroke:#d97706,color:#fbbf24
  classDef process fill:#2563eb20,stroke:#2563eb,color:#60a5fa
  classDef good fill:#05996020,stroke:#059669,color:#34d399
  class Alert,War,IC sev1
  class Sev,Root,OK sev2
  class Ack,Inv,Diag,Fix,Esc,Verify,Comms process
  class PIR,Actions,Ticket good

name: OAuth 2.0 PKCE Flow
description: Full PKCE authorization code flow: browser, auth server, resource server, token store.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor User
  participant Browser
  participant AS as Authorization Server
  participant RS as Resource Server
  participant TS as Token Store

  User->>Browser: Click Sign In
  Browser->>Browser: Generate code_verifier + code_challenge (S256)
  Browser->>AS: GET /authorize?response_type=code&code_challenge=...&scope=openid profile
  AS->>User: Render login + MFA prompt
  User->>AS: Submit credentials + OTP
  AS->>AS: Validate, create auth_code (TTL 60s)
  AS->>Browser: 302 to callback?code=auth_code&state=...
  Browser->>AS: POST /token {code, code_verifier, client_id}
  AS->>AS: Verify code_challenge == S256(code_verifier)
  AS->>TS: Store hashed refresh_token (TTL 90d)
  AS->>Browser: access_token JWT (15min) + refresh_token
  Browser->>RS: GET /api/resource Authorization: Bearer JWT
  RS->>RS: Verify JWT signature + expiry locally
  RS-->>Browser: 200 OK + data
  Browser->>User: Render protected content

  Note over Browser,AS: PKCE prevents authorization code injection attacks

name: Payment Processing Flow
description: End-to-end payment: customer, merchant, payment gateway, card network, bank.
code:
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor Customer
  participant Merchant
  participant Gateway as Payment Gateway
  participant Network as Card Network
  participant IssuerBank as Issuer Bank
  participant Ledger

  Customer->>Merchant: Submit payment (card + CVV)
  Merchant->>Gateway: POST /charge {amount, currency, card_token}
  Gateway->>Gateway: Tokenise PAN (PCI DSS)
  Gateway->>Network: Authorization Request (ISO 8583)
  Network->>IssuerBank: Forward auth request
  IssuerBank->>IssuerBank: Check balance, fraud score, 3DS if needed
  IssuerBank-->>Network: Auth response + approval_code
  Network-->>Gateway: Forwarded response
  Gateway-->>Merchant: {status: approved, auth_code}
  Merchant-->>Customer: Order confirmed

  Note over Gateway,Network: Settlement runs in batch T+1
  Gateway->>Network: Batch settlement file
  Network->>IssuerBank: Debit cardholder account
  IssuerBank->>Ledger: Post transaction
  Network-->>Gateway: Settlement confirmation
  Gateway-->>Merchant: Funds deposited

name: SaaS Multi-Tenant — C4 Container
description: Container diagram for a schema-per-tenant SaaS platform with BFF, identity, and async workers.
code:
C4Container
  title Container Diagram — SaaS Multi-Tenant Platform

  Person(admin, "Tenant Admin")
  Person(user, "End User")

  Container_Boundary(platform, "SaaS Platform") {
    Container(web, "Web Application", "React + TypeScript", "SPA served via CDN")
    Container(bff, "BFF / API Gateway", "Node.js", "Per-tenant routing, rate limiting, auth")
    Container(identity, "Identity Service", "Keycloak", "OIDC with tenant isolation")
    Container(core, "Core Service", "Java / Spring Boot", "Business logic, multi-tenant aware")
    Container(analytics, "Analytics Service", "Python / FastAPI", "Usage metrics, reporting")
    Container(worker, "Background Worker", "Go", "Async jobs, email, webhooks")
    ContainerDb(pg, "Tenant Database", "PostgreSQL (schema-per-tenant)", "Isolated data per tenant")
    ContainerDb(redis, "Cache / Session", "Redis Cluster", "Session tokens, feature flags")
    ContainerDb(s3, "Object Storage", "S3 / Minio", "Tenant file uploads")
  }

  Rel(admin, web, "Manages configuration", "HTTPS")
  Rel(user, web, "Uses application", "HTTPS")
  Rel(web, identity, "Authenticates", "OIDC")
  Rel(web, bff, "API calls", "JSON / HTTPS")
  Rel(bff, core, "Routes requests", "gRPC")
  Rel(bff, analytics, "Analytics queries", "REST")
  Rel(core, pg, "Reads/Writes", "JDBC")
  Rel(core, redis, "Caches", "Redis protocol")
  Rel(core, worker, "Enqueues jobs", "AMQP")
  Rel(worker, s3, "Stores files", "S3 API")

name: E-Commerce Data Model
description: Complete e-commerce ER schema: Customer, Order, Product, Payment, Category.
code:
erDiagram
  CUSTOMER {
    uuid id PK
    string email UK
    string full_name
    string phone
    timestamp created_at
    timestamp deleted_at
  }
  ADDRESS {
    uuid id PK
    uuid customer_id FK
    string line1
    string city
    string country_code
    string postal_code
    boolean is_default
  }
  CATEGORY {
    uuid id PK
    uuid parent_id FK
    string name
    string slug UK
  }
  PRODUCT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid category_id FK
    string sku UK
    string name
    numeric price
    int stock_qty
    boolean is_active
  }
  ORDER {
    uuid id PK
    uuid customer_id FK
    uuid shipping_address_id FK
    string status
    numeric total
    timestamp placed_at
    timestamp fulfilled_at
  }
  ORDER_LINE {
    uuid id PK
    uuid order_id FK
    uuid product_id FK
    int quantity
    numeric unit_price
    numeric line_total
  }
  PAYMENT {
    uuid id PK
    uuid order_id FK
    string provider
    string provider_ref UK
    string status
    numeric amount
    string currency
    timestamp paid_at
  }

  CUSTOMER ||--o{ ADDRESS : "has"
  CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : "places"
  ORDER ||--|{ ORDER_LINE : "contains"
  ORDER ||--|| ADDRESS : "ships to"
  ORDER ||--o{ PAYMENT : "paid via"
  ORDER_LINE }o--|| PRODUCT : "references"
  PRODUCT }o--|| CATEGORY : "belongs to"
  CATEGORY ||--o{ CATEGORY : "parent of"

name: Cloud Architecture — Architecture Beta
description: Native architecture-beta diagram: API, database, disk, server, and cloud groups.
code:
architecture-beta
  group api(cloud)[API Tier]
  group data(cloud)[Data Tier]

  service lb(internet)[Load Balancer]
  service app1(server)[App Server A] in api
  service app2(server)[App Server B] in api
  service cache(server)[Redis Cache] in api
  service db(database)[Primary DB] in data
  service replica(database)[Read Replica] in data
  service storage(disk)[Object Storage] in data

  lb:R --> L:app1
  lb:R --> L:app2
  app1:R --> L:cache
  app2:R --> L:cache
  app1:B --> T:db
  app2:B --> T:db
  db:R --> L:replica
  app1:B --> T:storage

name: Product Feature Journey
description: The full lifecycle story of a product feature — from spark of an idea to user feedback.
code:
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'themeVariables': {'primaryColor': '#1e293b', 'primaryTextColor': '#e2e8f0', 'lineColor': '#60a5fa', 'edgeLabelBackground': '#0f172a'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    A(["fa:fa-lightbulb  Idea Spark"]):::idea
    B["fa:fa-users  Stakeholder Workshop"]:::stake
    C{"fa:fa-question  Feasible?"}:::decision
    D["fa:fa-drafting-compass  UX Research & Wireframes"]:::design
    E["fa:fa-file-alt  PRD Approved"]:::doc
    F["fa:fa-sitemap  System Design Review"]:::arch
    G["fa:fa-code  Sprint Planning"]:::dev
    H["fa:fa-cogs  Development"]:::dev
    I["fa:fa-bug  QA & Testing"]:::test
    J{"fa:fa-check-circle  All Tests Pass?"}:::decision
    K["fa:fa-rocket  Production Deploy"]:::deploy
    L["fa:fa-chart-line  Metrics & Monitoring"]:::monitor
    M["fa:fa-comments  User Feedback"]:::feedback
    N(["fa:fa-trophy  Feature Success"]):::success
    O(["fa:fa-times-circle  Parking Lot"]):::rejected

    A --> B
    B --> C
    C -->|"fa:fa-thumbs-up Yes"| D
    C -->|"fa:fa-thumbs-down No"| O
    D --> E
    E --> F
    F --> G
    G --> H
    H --> I
    I --> J
    J -->|"fa:fa-redo Fix & Retest"| H
    J -->|"fa:fa-check Green"| K
    K --> L
    L --> M
    M -->|"fa:fa-arrow-up Positive"| N
    M -->|"fa:fa-sync Iterate"| B

    classDef idea fill:#7c3aed,stroke:#5b21b6,color:#fff
    classDef stake fill:#1e40af,stroke:#1d4ed8,color:#fff
    classDef decision fill:#92400e,stroke:#b45309,color:#fff
    classDef design fill:#065f46,stroke:#059669,color:#fff
    classDef doc fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#fff
    classDef arch fill:#4a1d96,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#fff
    classDef dev fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#60a5fa,color:#fff
    classDef test fill:#7f1d1d,stroke:#ef4444,color:#fff
    classDef deploy fill:#14532d,stroke:#22c55e,color:#fff
    classDef monitor fill:#0c4a6e,stroke:#0ea5e9,color:#fff
    classDef feedback fill:#3b0764,stroke:#a855f7,color:#fff
    classDef success fill:#14532d,stroke:#4ade80,color:#fff
    classDef rejected fill:#1f2937,stroke:#6b7280,color:#9ca3af

name: Requirements Collection Story
description: How requirements flow from vision to verified backlog — a sequence diagram story.
code:
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'themeVariables': {'actorBkg': '#1e293b', 'actorBorder': '#3b82f6', 'actorTextColor': '#e2e8f0', 'activationBkgColor': '#1e3a5f', 'noteBkgColor': '#0f172a', 'noteTextColor': '#94a3b8', 'messageFontSize': '13px'}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    actor CEO as 🏢 CEO / Sponsor
    participant PM as 📋 Product Manager
    participant UX as 🎨 UX Designer
    participant BA as 📊 Business Analyst
    participant Dev as 💻 Tech Lead
    participant QA as 🔍 QA Engineer

    Note over CEO,QA: Phase 1 — Vision & Discovery

    CEO->>PM: Share business vision & OKRs
    PM->>BA: Kick off requirements workshop
    BA->>CEO: Conduct stakeholder interviews
    CEO-->>BA: Pain points & success metrics

    Note over PM,UX: Phase 2 — Research & Ideation

    BA->>UX: Share raw requirements
    UX->>UX: Competitive analysis & user research
    UX-->>PM: User journey maps & personas
    PM->>Dev: Technical feasibility check
    Dev-->>PM: Constraints & complexity estimates

    Note over BA,QA: Phase 3 — Specification

    PM->>BA: Prioritise with MoSCoW method
    BA->>BA: Write functional specifications
    BA-->>Dev: Share system requirements
    BA-->>QA: Share acceptance criteria
    Dev->>Dev: Draft architecture decision records
    QA->>QA: Draft test scenarios

    Note over PM,QA: Phase 4 — Review & Sign-off

    PM->>CEO: Present PRD for approval
    CEO-->>PM: ✅ Sign off
    PM->>Dev: Sprint backlog ready
    PM->>QA: Test plan aligned
    Dev-->>PM: Estimation complete
    QA-->>PM: QA plan complete

    Note over CEO,QA: Phase 5 — Build starts 🚀

name: Agile Project Story
description: The complete Agile project lifecycle from inception to retrospective — as a visual story.
code:
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'themeVariables': {'primaryColor': '#1e293b', 'primaryTextColor': '#e2e8f0', 'lineColor': '#60a5fa'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    subgraph I ["fa:fa-flag  Inception"]
        direction TB
        I1["fa:fa-bullseye  Vision Statement"]
        I2["fa:fa-users  Core Team Assembled"]
        I3["fa:fa-map  Roadmap Draft"]
        I1 --> I2 --> I3
    end

    subgraph S0 ["fa:fa-layer-group  Sprint Zero"]
        direction TB
        S01["fa:fa-tools  Dev Environment Setup"]
        S02["fa:fa-sitemap  Architecture Spike"]
        S03["fa:fa-list  Backlog Populated"]
        S01 --> S02 --> S03
    end

    subgraph SP ["fa:fa-sync  Sprint Cycle x N"]
        direction TB
        SP1["fa:fa-calendar  Sprint Planning"]
        SP2["fa:fa-code  Daily Build"]
        SP3["fa:fa-comments  Daily Standup"]
        SP4["fa:fa-check-square  Sprint Review"]
        SP5["fa:fa-chart-bar  Retrospective"]
        SP1 --> SP2
        SP2 --> SP3
        SP3 --> SP2
        SP2 --> SP4
        SP4 --> SP5
    end

    subgraph REL ["fa:fa-rocket  Release"]
        direction TB
        R1["fa:fa-vial  UAT / Staging"]
        R2["fa:fa-shield-alt  Security Review"]
        R3["fa:fa-upload  Production Deploy"]
        R1 --> R2 --> R3
    end

    subgraph OPS ["fa:fa-heartbeat  Operate"]
        direction TB
        O1["fa:fa-chart-line  Monitoring"]
        O2["fa:fa-bug  Bug Triage"]
        O3["fa:fa-star  User Feedback"]
        O1 --> O2 --> O3
    end

    I --> S0 --> SP --> REL --> OPS
    OPS -->|"fa:fa-redo  Next Cycle"| SP

    style I fill:#1e1b4b,stroke:#6366f1,color:#c7d2fe
    style S0 fill:#1a2744,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#bfdbfe
    style SP fill:#14532d,stroke:#22c55e,color:#bbf7d0
    style REL fill:#451a03,stroke:#f97316,color:#fed7aa
    style OPS fill:#0c4a6e,stroke:#0ea5e9,color:#bae6fd

name: System Design Story
description: A system design interview walkthrough — from requirements to final architecture, told as a story.
code:
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'themeVariables': {'primaryColor': '#1e293b', 'primaryTextColor': '#e2e8f0', 'lineColor': '#60a5fa', 'edgeLabelBackground': '#0f172a'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    A(["fa:fa-comments  Interviewer Prompt"]):::start
    B["fa:fa-question-circle  Clarify Requirements
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
fa:fa-users  DAU / MAU?
fa:fa-hdd  Data volume?
fa:fa-globe  Global or regional?
fa:fa-bolt  Read vs Write ratio?"]:::req

    C["fa:fa-calculator  Capacity Estimation
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
fa:fa-database  Storage: 500TB/yr
fa:fa-network-wired  Bandwidth: 10Gbps
fa:fa-server  Servers: ~200 nodes"]:::est

    D["fa:fa-sitemap  High-Level Design"]:::hld

    subgraph Core ["fa:fa-cogs  Core Architecture"]
        direction LR
        LB["fa:fa-balance-scale  Load Balancer"]
        GW["fa:fa-door-open  API Gateway"]
        SVC["fa:fa-cubes  Microservices"]
        CACHE["fa:fa-bolt  Redis Cache"]
        DB[("fa:fa-database  Primary DB")]
        CDN["fa:fa-cloud  CDN"]
        MQ["fa:fa-envelope  Message Queue"]
        LB --> GW --> SVC
        SVC --> CACHE
        SVC --> DB
        SVC --> MQ
        CDN --> LB
    end

    E["fa:fa-search-plus  Deep Dive
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
fa:fa-lock  Auth: JWT + OAuth2
fa:fa-copy  Replication: leader-follower
fa:fa-shield-alt  Rate limiting: token bucket
fa:fa-chart-bar  Sharding: by user_id hash"]:::deep

    F["fa:fa-exclamation-triangle  Bottlenecks & Trade-offs
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
fa:fa-sync  Consistency vs Availability
fa:fa-expand  Vertical vs Horizontal scale
fa:fa-compress  Normalise vs Denormalise"]:::tradeoff

    G(["fa:fa-trophy  Design Accepted"]):::success

    A --> B --> C --> D --> Core --> E --> F --> G

    classDef start fill:#4c1d95,stroke:#7c3aed,color:#ede9fe
    classDef req fill:#1e3a5f,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#bfdbfe
    classDef est fill:#064e3b,stroke:#059669,color:#a7f3d0
    classDef hld fill:#1e293b,stroke:#60a5fa,color:#e2e8f0
    classDef deep fill:#1c1917,stroke:#a8a29e,color:#d6d3d1
    classDef tradeoff fill:#451a03,stroke:#f97316,color:#fed7aa
    classDef success fill:#14532d,stroke:#4ade80,color:#bbf7d0
    style Core fill:#0f172a,stroke:#334155

name: Bug to Production Story
description: The journey of a critical production bug — from alert to postmortem, told as a sequence.
code:
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'themeVariables': {'actorBkg': '#1e293b', 'actorBorder': '#ef4444', 'actorTextColor': '#e2e8f0', 'noteBkgColor': '#1a0000', 'noteTextColor': '#fca5a5'}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    actor Alert as 🚨 PagerDuty
    participant SRE as 🔧 SRE On-Call
    participant Slack as 💬 Incident Slack
    participant Dev as 💻 Developer
    participant DB as 🗄️ Database
    participant CEO as 🏢 Leadership

    Note over Alert,CEO: 🔴 Incident declared — P1 Production Outage

    Alert->>SRE: fa:fa-bell ALERT: Error rate 45% — checkout service
    SRE->>Slack: fa:fa-fire Create #incident-2024-042
    SRE->>CEO: Executive notification sent

    Note over SRE,Dev: 🔍 Triage & Investigation

    SRE->>Dev: Page on-call developer
    Dev->>DB: fa:fa-search Query slow_query_log
    DB-->>Dev: fa:fa-table N+1 query detected in OrderService
    Dev->>Dev: fa:fa-code-branch Checkout git blame
    Dev-->>SRE: Root cause: missing index on orders.user_id

    Note over SRE,DB: 🛠️ Mitigation

    SRE->>DB: fa:fa-bolt Apply hotfix index CONCURRENTLY
    DB-->>SRE: fa:fa-check Index created — query: 2s → 12ms
    SRE->>Slack: fa:fa-arrow-up Error rate recovering: 45% → 2%

    Note over SRE,Dev: ✅ Resolution

    SRE->>CEO: fa:fa-check-circle Incident resolved — 38 min MTTR
    Dev->>Dev: fa:fa-file-alt Write postmortem
    Dev-->>Slack: fa:fa-calendar Schedule blameless retro

    Note over Alert,CEO: 📋 Action Items: add DB query budget alerts, migrate to ORM with N+1 detection
